about: Hand-annotated authoritative chord changes for a small set of harmonically-clear sections, used to measure the src/analysis harmonic analyzer (Phase 1) against the crude pooled-bag inferChord baseline, MIREX-style (root / maj-min / full-quality, duration-weighted). Changes are expressed in SECTION-RELATIVE quarter-note beats (0 = the section's first downbeat).
provenance: Every change was derived from the section's ACTUAL MIDI pitch content in this library (the committed .mid files), NOT transcribed from a copyrighted lead sheet. Public-domain / traditional pieces are preferred; the two pop sections' changes are the well-known factual progressions confirmed against the pitch content.
honestCaveats: Small (6 sections, ~24 measures) and SINGLE-ANNOTATOR — there is no inter-annotator agreement check. Expert agreement on chord labels is only ~76% root / ~73% maj-min / ~54% on complex qualities (Koops et al. 2019; Ni et al. 2013), so these numbers are a relative A/B signal, never an absolute grade of correctness.

The Bach prelude is included deliberately as the analyzer's HARD case (a pure fast arpeggio, which beat-synchronous segmentation fragments — the Session-2 HCDF target); its changes are textbook-unambiguous, so it is a fair, honest stress test, not a cherry-pick.

sections.label: BWV846 mm.1-7 — the textbook arpeggiated progression (analyzer's hard case; baseline is blind, left hand is empty)
sections.provenance: public domain (J.S. Bach); one chord per measure, unambiguous from the arpeggiated content

sections.label: mm.3-6 — a sustained E-minor vamp (the left hand is empty, so the pooled-left-hand baseline cannot label it at all)
sections.provenance: traditional Andean melody (public domain); a right-hand E-G-B tremolo over an empty left hand, held Em with melodic passing tones

sections.label: mm.1-2 — the famous opening (Alberti figuration)
sections.provenance: public domain (W.A. Mozart); tonic then dominant-7th

sections.label: mm.1-2 — modal D-minor opening (6/8, arpeggiated bass)
sections.provenance: traditional English ballad (public domain); a D-F-A bass arpeggio, unambiguous Dm

sections.label: mm.5-6 — hymn cadence (tonic then subdominant)
sections.provenance: traditional Shaker tune (public domain); C then F from the block content

sections.label: mm.2-5 — the iconic block-chord verse progression, two chords per bar (the analyzer's common-case strength)
sections.provenance: the factual C-G-Am-F-C-G-F-C progression, confirmed against the per-half-measure bass + chord content in the library's MIDI

sections.label: mm.2-6 — the block-chord Cmaj7/F vamp (another clean block-chord case for the analyzer's strong suit)
sections.provenance: the factual Cmaj7-F alternation, confirmed against the per-measure left-hand chord content

sections.label: mm.2-3 — a held Em with G in the bass (inversion stressor: bass ≠ root, the pedal-root failure mode)
sections.provenance: public domain (Chopin, Op.28 No.4 opening); {E,G,B} = Em, sounded with G lowest

sections.label: mm.3-4 — Eb then Bbm, both in inversion (block-chord pop, bass ≠ root)
sections.provenance: the factual Eb-Bbm move, confirmed against the content ({Eb,G,Bb} over G; {Bb,Db,F} over F)
